1→"""Stack landing logic for `erk land --stack`. 2→ 3→Lands an entire Graphite stack bottom-up: validate all PRs, then merge 4→each from the bottom, rebasing upstack branches between iterations. 5→ 6→This module is separate from land_cmd.py because the stack loop is 7→fundamentally different from the single-PR pipeline pattern. 8→""" 9→ 10→from dataclasses import dataclass 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→import click 14→ 15→from erk.cli.commands.land_cmd import check_unresolved_comments 16→from erk.cli.commands.objective_helpers import ( 17→ get_objective_for_branch, 18→ run_objective_update_after_land, 19→) 20→from erk.cli.commands.wt.delete_cmd import _prune_worktrees_safe 21→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 22→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 23→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import MergeError, PRDetails, PRNotFound 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→ 28→@dataclass(frozen=True) 29→class StackLandEntry: 30→ """Pre-validated entry for a single branch in a stack land operation."""

tool_use.name: TodoWrite
tool_use.param: [{'content': 'Create src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py with core stack landing logic', 's...[truncated 415 chars]...I checks (ty, ruff, pytest)', 'status': 'in_progress', 'activeForm': 'Running CI checks'}]

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:
1. The user triggered `/erk:plan-implement 8948` which is an automated implementation workflow.
2. The workflow set up implementation for plan #8948, which is about implementing `erk land --stack` to land an entire Graphite stack bottom-up.
3. I ran `erk exec setup-impl --issue 8948` to set up the implementation environment.
4. I read the plan from `.erk/impl-context/plnd--land-stack-graphite-03-08-0127/plan.md`.
5. I signaled implementation started and launched an Explore agent to gather comprehensive codebase context about: - land_cmd.py structure - Gateway interfaces (BranchManager, Git, GitHub) - Fake implementations for testing - Test patterns - Data types (PRDetails, RebaseResult, etc.)
6. The explore agent returned very detailed findings about all the gateway interfaces, types, and test patterns.
7. I loaded the dignified-python skill for coding standards.
8. I read multiple files to understand: - FakeBranchManager - FakeLocalGitHub (fake.py) - FakeGitRebaseOps - Existing land tests (test_merge_pr.py, test_core.py) - context_for_test function - ErkContext class and its branch_manager property - FakeGraphite and FakeGraphiteBranchOps - Various gateway ABCs
9. Key discovery: `ctx.branch_manager` is a computed property on ErkContext that derives from the `graphite` configuration. You can't set `_branch_manager` directly. Instead, you must pass `graphite=FakeGraphite(stacks={...})` to `context_for_test`, which automatically creates linked branch ops.
10. I created `src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py` with the core stack landing logic.
11. I modified `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` to add the `--stack` flag and routing.
12. I initially created tests using `FakeBranchManager` and `ctx._branch_manager = fake_branch_manager`, which was wrong. I discovered the error by reading the ErkContext source and understanding the `branch_manager` property is computed from graphite.
13. I rewrote the tests to use `FakeGraphite` with stacks configured, which is the proper pattern.
14. I was about to run CI checks when the summary was requested.
Key files created/modified: - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py` (NEW) - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` (MODIFIED) - `tests/unit/cli/commands/land/test_land_stack.py` (NEW)
Important patterns discovered: - Tests use `context_for_test()` from `erk.core.context` - FakeGraphite accepts `stacks` dict for stack configuration - `context_for_test` auto-creates linked branch ops when given FakeGraphite - `Ensure.invariant(False, msg)` raises SystemExit for error handling - Tests catch SystemExit to verify error cases - FakeLocalGitHub tracks mutations via `merged_prs`, `updated_pr_bases`, `deleted_remote_branches`, `operation_log` - FakeGit tracks `rebase_onto_calls`, `rebase_abort_calls`, `pushed_branches`, `fetched_branches`, `deleted_branches`
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user triggered `/erk:plan-implement 8948` which is an automated workflow to implement plan #8948: adding `erk land --stack` functionality to land an entire Graphite stack bottom-up. The plan involves: - Adding a `--stack` CLI flag to the existing `erk land` command - Creating a new `land_stack.py` module with the core stack landing algorithm - The algorithm: resolve stack → pre-validate all PRs → for each branch bottom-up: rebase onto trunk, re-parent upstack PRs, merge, delete remote branch → cleanup - Writing comprehensive unit tests using the project's fake-driven testing pattern
2. Key Technical Concepts: - Graphite stack management (branch stacks ordered trunk-to-leaf) - Bottom-up stack landing algorithm (merge closest-to-trunk first) - PR re-parenting before merge (update child PR base branches to trunk to prevent GitHub auto-close) - Rebase-and-push between iterations - Fake-driven testing pattern (no mock.patch, constructor injection) - ErkContext computed property pattern (`branch_manager` derives from `graphite` config) - Frozen dataclass state threading with `dataclasses.replace()` - `Ensure.invariant()` for user-facing error handling (raises SystemExit) - Two-pipeline land pattern (validation + execution) - `context_for_test()` auto-wiring of FakeGraphite → linked FakeGraphiteBranchOps
3. Files and Code Sections:
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py`** (CREATED - core implementation) - New module containing the stack landing logic, separate from land_cmd.py - Key types: `StackLandEntry` (frozen dataclass with branch, pr_number, pr_details, worktree_path, plan_id, objective_number) - Key functions: `execute_land_stack()`, `_resolve_stack()`, `_validate_stack_prs()`, `_reparent_upstack()`, `_rebase_and_push()`, `_merge_and_cleanup_branch()`, `_cleanup_after_stack_land()`, `_pull_trunk()` - Full file is 370 lines, algorithm: resolve stack → validate → display summary → loop (rebase, reparent, merge) → cleanup → pull trunk → objective updates ```python def execute_land_stack(ctx: ErkContext, *, repo: RepoContext, force: bool, pull_flag: bool, no_delete: bool, skip_learn: bool) -> None: # Step 1: Resolve stack branches_to_land = _resolve_stack(ctx, main_repo_root) # Step 2: Pre-validate all PRs entries = _validate_stack_prs(ctx, main_repo_root, branches_to_land, trunk, force=force) # Step 5: Land each branch bottom-up for i, entry in enumerate(entries): if i > 0: _rebase_and_push(ctx, main_repo_root, entry.branch, trunk) remaining = entries[i + 1:] if remaining: _reparent_upstack(ctx, main_repo_root, remaining, trunk) _merge_and_cleanup_branch(ctx, main_repo_root, entry) # ... cleanup, pull trunk, objective updates raise SystemExit(0) ```
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py`** (MODIFIED - added --stack flag) - Added `--stack` click option before `--skip-learn` - Added `stack_flag: bool` parameter to `land()` function - Added mutual exclusion checks: `--stack` vs `--up`/`--down`, `--stack` vs target argument - Added dispatch: `if stack_flag: from erk.cli.commands.land_stack import execute_land_stack; execute_land_stack(...); return` - The dispatch is placed BEFORE the validation pipeline, as --stack has its own validation flow ```python @click.option("--stack", "stack_flag", is_flag=True, help="Land the entire Graphite stack bottom-up.") # ... in land() function body: Ensure.invariant(not (stack_flag and (up_flag or down_flag)), "--stack is mutually exclusive with --up and --down.") Ensure.invariant(not (stack_flag and target is not None), "--stack cannot be combined with a target argument.") if stack_flag: from erk.cli.commands.land_stack import execute_land_stack execute_land_stack(ctx, repo=repo, force=force, pull_flag=pull_flag, no_delete=no_delete, skip_learn=skip_learn) return ```
- **`tests/unit/cli/commands/land/test_land_stack.py`** (CREATED - unit tests) - Tests organized into classes: TestResolveStack, TestValidateStackPrs, TestReparentUpstack, TestMergeAndCleanupBranch, TestRebaseAndPush, TestExecuteLandStack - Uses `FakeGraphite(stacks={...})` for stack configuration (NOT FakeBranchManager) - Uses `context_for_test(git=..., github=..., graphite=..., cwd=...)` for test context - Test cases: happy path 3-branch stack, single branch, rebase conflict, merge failure, no_delete preserves branches - Helper functions: `_make_pr_details()`, `_make_pull_request_info()`, `_make_repo()`
- **Key files READ for understanding:** - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py` - ErkContext class, `branch_manager` property (computed from graphite config) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/fake.py` - FakeGraphite with `stacks` parameter and `create_linked_branch_ops()` - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/branch_ops/fake.py` - FakeGraphiteBranchOps with track_branch_calls tracking - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py` - FakeLocalGitHub with mutation tracking (merged_prs, updated_pr_bases, deleted_remote_branches, operation_log) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/fake.py` - FakeGit constructor parameters (worktrees, current_branches, default_branches, rebase_onto_result, etc.) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py` - PRDetails, MergeResult, MergeError, PullRequestInfo types - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/abc.py` - RebaseResult, BranchDivergence types - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/branch_manager/graphite.py` - GraphiteBranchManager (track_branch delegates to graphite_branch_ops) - `src/erk/core/context.py` - `context_for_test()` function (auto-creates linked branch ops for FakeGraphite at line 335-337) - `tests/unit/cli/commands/land/pipeline/test_merge_pr.py` - Test pattern reference - `tests/commands/land/test_core.py` - CLI-level test pattern reference - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` - Full land command (1636+ lines), `check_unresolved_comments()` function signature
4. Errors and fixes: - **FakeBranchManager with ctx._branch_manager assignment**: Initially wrote tests using `FakeBranchManager` and tried to set `ctx._branch_manager = fake_branch_manager`. Discovered this doesn't work because `branch_manager` is a computed property on ErkContext that derives from `self.graphite`. Fixed by switching to `FakeGraphite(stacks={...})` passed to `context_for_test()`, which auto-creates linked `FakeGraphiteBranchOps`. - **Write before Read error**: Attempted to rewrite `test_land_stack.py` without reading it first, got a tool error. Fixed by reading the file first then writing.
5. Problem Solving: - Solved: How to properly configure test contexts with Graphite stack awareness (use FakeGraphite, not FakeBranchManager) - Solved: Understanding the relationship between ErkContext.branch_manager → graphite → graphite_branch_ops → FakeGraphiteBranchOps chain - Solved: Understanding that `context_for_test` at line 335 auto-detects FakeGraphite and calls `create_linked_branch_ops()` which links mutation tracking - Solved: Understanding that `detect_trunk_branch` defaults to "main" in FakeGitBranchOps when no trunk_branches configured - Solved: Understanding that FakeLocalGitHub uses `prs_by_branch` dict for `get_pr_for_branch()` lookups
6. All user messages: - The user triggered `/erk:plan-implement 8948` command which provided the full implementation workflow instructions - The user's system provided the dignified-python skill content when the Skill tool was invoked - No direct user feedback messages were received; the workflow is automated
7. Pending Tasks: - Run CI checks (ty type checker, ruff linter, pytest) - this was the immediate next step - The tests have NOT been run yet - there may be compilation/import errors or test failures - Submit PR via `erk pr submit` after CI passes - Signal lifecycle transitions (`impl-signal ended`, `upload-impl-session`, `cleanup-impl-context`) - CLI-level tests (tests/commands/land/test_stack_flag.py) were planned but deprioritized
8. Current Work: I had just finished creating the unit tests at `tests/unit/cli/commands/land/test_land_stack.py` (the second rewrite using FakeGraphite instead of FakeBranchManager) and updated the todo list to mark "Run CI checks" as `in_progress`. The tests haven't been executed yet. The implementation has three files: - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py` - new module with stack landing logic - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` - modified with --stack flag - `tests/unit/cli/commands/land/test_land_stack.py` - unit tests
9. Optional Next Step: Run CI checks (pytest for the new tests, ty type checker, ruff linter) to verify the implementation compiles and tests pass. The todo list shows "Run CI checks (ty, ruff, pytest)" as `in_progress`. Per the plan-implement workflow Step 12: "If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands.". Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

1→"""Stack landing logic for `erk land --stack`. 2→ 3→Lands an entire Graphite stack bottom-up: validate all PRs, then merge 4→each from the bottom, rebasing upstack branches between iterations. 5→ 6→This module is separate from land_cmd.py because the stack loop is 7→fundamentally different from the single-PR pipeline pattern. 8→""" 9→ 10→from dataclasses import dataclass 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→import click 14→ 15→from erk.cli.commands.land_cmd import check_unresolved_comments 16→from erk.cli.commands.objective_helpers import ( 17→ get_objective_for_branch, 18→ run_objective_update_after_land, 19→) 20→from erk.cli.commands.wt.delete_cmd import _prune_worktrees_safe 21→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 22→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 23→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import MergeError, PRDetails, PRNotFound 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→ 28→@dataclass(frozen=True) 29→class StackLandEntry: 30→ """Pre-validated entry for a single branch in a stack land operation."""
... [345 lines omitted] ...
263→ Bails with error if rebase has conflicts. 322→ not isinstance(merge_result, MergeError), 323→ f"Failed to merge PR #{entry.pr_number} [{entry.branch}]: " 324→ + (merge_result.message if isinstance(merge_result, MergeError) else ""), 366→ except RuntimeError: 368→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + "git pull failed (try running manually)"
session.assistant: Now I need to look at what `Ensure.invariant` actually raises to understand the exception type:
session.tool_result: 59: def invariant(condition: bool, error_message: str) -> None: 60- """Ensure condition is true, otherwise output styled error and exit. 61- 62- Args: 63- condition: Boolean condition to check 64- error_message: Error message to display if condition is false. 65- "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 66- 67- Raises: 68- UserFacingCliError: If condition is false 69- """

src/erk/cli/ensure.py:33:class UserFacingCliError(click.ClickException): /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/ensure.py-34- """Exception for user-facing CLI errors with styled output. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/ensure.py-35- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/ensure.py-36- Extends click.ClickException so Click catches it automatically at every
session.assistant:
`Ensure.invariant` raises `UserFacingCliError` (which extends `click.ClickException`), not `SystemExit`. The tests that expect `SystemExit` for `Ensure.invariant` failures need to be changed to `UserFacingCliError`. However, `execute_land_stack` explicitly `raise SystemExit(0)` on success, so those tests are correct.
Let me update the todo list and fix all three issues:
